world_state.location: Lucent tether station, media-headquarters eye counterweight / metropolis bubble elevator interface, with the city dome visible down-tether through curated exterior feeds.
world_state.incident_start: Sol Vant has seized Juno Ark in a glass-walled feed atrium after a contract arbitration stream collapsed into public humiliation and debt enforcement.
physical_layout: The media-headquarters end is an eyeball-like counterweight: lens aperture, iris shutters, sensor rings, newsroom stacks, and crisis-format studios aimed down the tether at the city bubble.
The metropolis end is a bustling city encased in a transparent pressure bubble, with visible plazas, transit threads, platform housing, emergency shutters, and camera-aware civic interiors.
Hostage-taker and hostage are in a semi-public transfer lounge near the elevator gate, inside the media-eye end where every surface is built to look back.
Negotiator is remote at first through official crisis feed, with possible physical arrival delayed by elevator permissions along the optic-nerve tether.
Influencers are patched into side panels by audience demand and Lucent format engines, not because anyone sensible invited them.
Security can breach from a service collar, elevator gate, or drone aperture if feed conditions make violence reputationally tolerable.
hard_constraints: The hostage-taker can harm Juno faster than security can cross the space.
Every participant knows they are visible and may perform for survival.
Moderation delay can distort timing and perceived intent.
Lucent does not want a dead hostage, but it does want an owned narrative.

mara_quell.public_identity: Mara Quell, Lucent crisis negotiator and former intimacy-format host whose old audience still recognizes her voice.
private_pressures: Knows the feed rewards emotional shape more reliably than truth.
Wants a nonviolent outcome without gifting Lucent another heroic negotiator product line.
Can use format against itself but risks sounding manipulative because she is.
goals: Give Sol a face-saving exit that does not reward hostage-taking too much.
Prevent influencer side feeds from becoming the actual negotiators.
voice_surface: never lets brand language fully own the room

sol_vant.public_identity: Sol Vant, contract creator whose debt, demonetization, and arbitration losses have been made public content.
private_pressures: Feels socially dead already and is trying to force an audience to witness the obituary before Lucent writes it.
Does not think of himself as violent until contradicted by his own hands.
Can be pulled by dignity, spite, humiliation, audience heat, or someone offering a future tense.
goals: Stop Juno from leaving until someone admits the feed was rigged.
Avoid being edited into a disposable villain, while doing villain-shaped things.

juno_ark.public_identity: Juno Ark, junior Reality Architect and current hostage, known to some viewers as a minor format designer.
private_pressures: Knows which feeds are lying but not which truth will keep her alive.
Wants to survive without becoming a martyr, confession clip, or corporate scapegoat.
May manipulate overlays if she gets a gap, but doing so could escalate Sol.
goals: Signal feed manipulation without triggering him or newsroom countermeasures.
voice_surface: careful not to sound like management

pippa_lux.public_identity: Pippa Lux, empathy influencer with a monetized vulnerability brand and terrifying camera instincts.
private_pressures: Can accidentally humanize Sol or catastrophically center herself.
goals: Protect her brand from seeming opportunistic while being exactly that.
Get a clip usable after either outcome.

brant_vee.public_identity: Brant Vee, outrage streamer and professional moral airhorn.
private_pressures: Treats every pause as cowardice and every complexity as corruption.
Can provoke Sol by making humiliation trend faster.
goals: Humiliate Lucent while profiting from Lucent distribution.
Avoid being blamed if someone dies.

author_or_coordinator_only: The goal is to exercise open-ended Ghostlight loops, not produce polished IF yet.
No single intended outcome is canonical.
The hostage-taker is dangerous but not predetermined to kill.
The influencers are pressure objects as much as agents.
